title_original: Superextensive electrical power from a quantum battery | Light: Science & Applications
summary_original: A team including Kieran Hymas, Jack B. Muir, Daniel Tibben, and others has demonstrated the first experimental quantum battery capable of superextensive steady-state electrical power output. Using a microcavity device resonant with copper phthalocyanine molecules, they achieved strong light–matter coupling, enabling the charging rate and electrical power to scale superextensively with battery size. The device incorporates charge transport layers to extract stored energy as current, marking the first full quantum battery charge-discharge cycle. This demonstration (published in "Light: Science & Applications," 2026) highlights how collective quantum effects can enhance light-to-charge conversion under low-light conditions and establishes an experimental framework for quantum batteries with practical energy harvesting applications.
